page: Amazon Web Services c8id.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 384 vCPUs, derived from 192 physical cores with hyper-threading, utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix and vector operations. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-capacity, low-latency local storage directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The hardware configuration lacks dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on the high-core-count CPU architecture for parallel processing tasks.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8id.96xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth (achieving over 1,140 GB/sec for large block reads), and database operations. It excels in software compilation, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression. However, single-core performance is average, and text generation speeds for large language models are limited compared to GPU-accelerated alternatives. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides high compute density and massive bundled local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage attachments for data-intensive workloads.

The c8id.96xlarge is highly suited for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, high-throughput web serving, and distributed database operations such as Redis. It is also capable of handling CPU-based machine learning prompt processing, though it is less optimal for sustained text generation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 384 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), delivering top-tier multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database operation speeds in benchmark tests. With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps and no integrated GPUs, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy, compute-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale software compilation, high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and distributed database systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 384 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and HPC workloads.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 384 Intel vCPUs, rapid DDR5 memory, and massive local NVMe storage for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
22,800 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD instance storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and databases
